title: Wonder and value of the Cango Caves highlighted as world celebrates Caves Week
description: OUDTSHOORN NEWS - The first week of June is...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"Gallery\n Video","html":"Gallery\n Video"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - The first week of June is globally recognised as Caves Week, with Caves Day celebrated annually on 6 June. This week not only celebrates the fascinating and mysterious underground world of caves, but also raises awareness about the importance of cave conservation, tourism and their scientific and cultural value.","html":"OUDTSHOORN NEWS - The first week of June is globally recognised as Caves Week, with Caves Day celebrated annually on 6 June. This week not only celebrates the fascinating and mysterious underground world of caves, but also raises awareness about the importance of cave conservation, tourism and their scientific and cultural value."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Caves Week brings international attention to one of the country's most iconic natural treasures, the Cango Caves.","html":"Caves Week brings international attention to one of the country's most iconic natural treasures, the Cango Caves."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Nestled in the foothills of the Swartberg mountains just outside Oudtshoorn, these awe-inspiring limestone caves are among South Africa's oldest tourist attractions and welcome thousands of visitors each year."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Caves","html":"The Caves"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Cango Caves are not only a geological marvel but also a vital part of South Africa's natural and historical heritage. Their breathtaking formations of stalactites and stalagmites, shaped over millions of years, offer a window into the Earth's deep history. The 20 million-year-old Cango Caves system consists of a series of hidden chambers and became a popular attraction during the 1800s. It is also the biggest show cave system in Africa.","html":"The Cango Caves are not only a geological marvel but also a vital part of South Africa's natural and historical heritage. Their breathtaking formations of stalactites and stalagmites, shaped over millions of years, offer a window into the Earth's deep history. The 20 million-year-old Cango Caves system consists of a series of hidden chambers and became a popular attraction during the 1800s. It is also the biggest show cave system in Africa."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"VIDEO - Inside the Cango Caves: Celebrating Caves Week at South Africa’s underground wonder"},{"type":"blockquote","text":"GALLERY: National Caves Week Celebrated"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"With increasing concerns around climate change and environmental degradation, Caves Week also serves as a crucial reminder of the need to protect and preserve caves like the Cango Caves. These environments are delicate and often home to rare species, microorganisms, and important archaeological and paleontological remains.","html":"With increasing concerns around climate change and environmental degradation, Caves Week also serves as a crucial reminder of the need to protect and preserve caves like the Cango Caves. These environments are delicate and often home to rare species, microorganisms, and important archaeological and paleontological remains."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"\"Caves Week is an important time for us to reflect on the true value of the Cango Caves, not just as a breathtaking natural wonder, but as a vital site for biodiversity, scientific research, and cultural heritage,\" said Alison Moos, manager of the Cango Caves."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"These caves are a national treasure that must be preserved for future generations. It is our responsibility to protect them against vandalism, irresponsible tourism, and any harm that could threaten their fragile ecosystem and historical integrity.\"","html":"\"These caves are a national treasure that must be preserved for future generations. It is our responsibility to protect them against vandalism, irresponsible tourism, and any harm that could threaten their fragile ecosystem and historical integrity.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The caves draw visitors from across the world and for Oudtshoorn and surrounding areas, the Cango Caves play a vital role in supporting the local economy and tourism industry.","html":"The caves draw visitors from across the world and for Oudtshoorn and surrounding areas, the Cango Caves play a vital role in supporting the local economy and tourism industry."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Wonder and value of the Cango Caves highlighted as world celebrates Caves Week
